content: set the default zoom level for the map to much closer - let's say some 30nm viewport max

Let me look at the current map setup to understand how zoom is configured.

Let me check the JavaScript asset for the current map zoom configuration.

Let me get the JS source and handler to see the zoom setup.

Let me read the JS asset source to find the map initialization and zoom level.

Now I can see the map uses zoom level 8 in three places. At zoom 8, the viewport shows ~300-400nm — way too wide. For ~30nm, I need around zoom 11 (gives ~30-40nm at typical sailing latitudes).

Let me get the full JS source to make the update.

I can see the map defaults to zoom level 8 in three places, which gives a viewport of ~300-400nm — way too wide. For a ~30nm viewport, zoom level 11 is the right fit (gives ~30-40nm at typical sailing latitudes).
if the value is in 3 locations, that's not DRY - it should be abstracted away in a const and used everywhere

Good call — let me revise the plan to extract the zoom level into a single constant.

Updated plan: add a single `const DEFAULT_ZOOM = 11;` at the top of the JS file and reference it in all 3 locations. One constant, one place to change it.

content: Approved. Go ahead with the plan.

Code change: Updated asset "sailing-weather.js"

Why: Extract hardcoded zoom level into a single constant for DRY compliance and easier future adjustments.
Implementation: Add a DEFAULT_ZOOM constant at the top of the IIFE and reference it in all three locations (initMap, handleGetLocation, tryGeolocation) instead of the hardcoded value 8.

Let me get the current JS source to make the update.

Now I have the full source. I can see the three hardcoded `8` values. Let me add a `DEFAULT_ZOOM` constant and replace all three.

Now let me mark the step done and complete the changeset.

Done. Added `var DEFAULT_ZOOM = 11;` in a new **Constants** section at the top of the IIFE, and all three zoom references (`initMap()`, `handleGetLocation()`, `tryGeolocation()`) now use `DEFAULT_ZOOM` instead of a hardcoded value. One line to change if you ever want to adjust the zoom level.
